transcript: When my parents look back at my childhood, they always describe my sister as the mischievous one or the troublemaker of the family, and they always describe me as the shant child of the family. But I don't know when this shant, shy girl turned into a scared and anxious girl. I am one of those many people who get scared to do basic normal everyday things. Like, um, if I'm at a restaurant, I used to get scared to order for a glass of water and let alone food, ordering food was like something out of a nightmare for me. So I would always ask my friends to do it. I hate answering phone calls. I don't. If I see an unknown number flashing on my phone, I'm just like, not happening. You gotta text me, I'm not gonna answer that call. And if I knew the answer in class, I would never put my hand up and answer because of the ever present self-doubt that whatever I know is wrong.
